content.problemStatement:
Given a `N x M` matrix, print its element in zig-zag fashion, i.e., print first row from left to right, second row from right to left, third row again from left to right and so on.

For eg., for the following matrix :



Output should be: 4 6 0 1 2 8 3 1 5


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the no. of rows and columns in the input matrix.
Next NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of the matrix.


Output Format

Output on a single line, N∗MN*MN∗M elements of the given matrix in zig-zag fashion.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two integers N and M denoting rows and columns, followed by N lines each containing M space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N·M integers representing the matrix traversed in zig‑zag order (first row left→right, second row right→left, and so on). Elements are separated by a single space with no trailing space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Row 0 (even) left→right: 4 6 0. Row 1 (odd) right→left: 1 2 8. Row 2 (even) left→right: 3 1 5. Concatenated result matches the output.
First row left→right gives 1 2. Second row right→left gives 4 3.

solution.approach: We read the matrix row by row. For even‑indexed rows (0‑based) we output the elements from left to right; for odd‑indexed rows we output them from right to left. This can be done in a single pass over the matrix, appending each element to the answer string while taking care of spaces. The algorithm is linear in the number of elements, which is optimal because every element must be printed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·M) per test case, O(total elements) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N·M) for storing the matrix (or O(M) if processed row by row).
